blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Juneteenth Celebration – City Springs**
📅 Coming to City Springs, Sandy Springs
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/27/juneteenth-celebration-city-springs/)

City Springs just announced this year's Juneteenth Celebration is coming back — and it's bigger. The event features live performances, local vendors, and expanded educational experiences that are new this year.

Juneteenth has grown into one of the most meaningful civic celebrations on the calendar, and City Springs is one of the best outdoor venues in the area to experience it. Free admission, walkable location, and a lineup built around community — it's the kind of event that reminds you why you like living here.

Details are still rolling out, so check cityssprings.com to mark your calendar before the full schedule fills in.
scoring_notes: Solid community event at a neighborhood anchor venue. Free admission removes any price barrier for an audience that wouldn't be deterred by cost anyway. The 'expanded' element gives it a fresh angle over prior years. Broad appeal across the adult and family mix in the Perimeter area. Slightly lower uniqueness score because Juneteenth events are widespread in metro Atlanta — the City Springs angle and local proximity is what elevates it for this newsletter specifically.

blurb: Mother's Day is May 10, and Roswell is making the whole weekend count with a garden tour just across the county line. It's a self-paced, outdoor event — the kind that feels like a legitimate outing without requiring a full-day commitment. Great for treating a mom who appreciates something a little more personal than brunch. Great for being the mom who actually wants to go somewhere beautiful on her own weekend. Pair it with lunch in historic Roswell and you've got a whole afternoon.
scoring_notes: The Mother's Day timing gives this real urgency and emotional relevance for an audience that skews 42, homeowning, and established — many of whom are either moms or buying experiences for moms. Garden tours appeal strongly to this demographic's lifestyle and income level. Docked on uniqueness because the details in the candidate listing are thin (community post, not a major organized event), and it may be smaller-scale than it sounds. Included as third pick because the timing hook is strong and it fills a 'what to do for Mother's Day' need the other two events don't address.

event_name: Springfest at Round Trip Brewing Co.
venue: Round Trip Brewing Co., East Cobb
blurb: Spring finally showed up, and Round Trip Brewing is throwing a party for it. Springfest hits the East Cobb location on May 9 with German-inspired beers, food, craft activities, and live music rolling in as the sun goes down. It's a patio day done right — the kind where you show up for an hour and somehow stay for three. No tickets, no fuss. Just grab someone worth spending a Saturday with and head over.
scoring_notes: Strong audience match for the East Cobb crowd — established adults, empty nesters, and parents of teens who want a low-key, walkable afternoon out. German beer and live music skew adult, which fits well. Free entry with purchase keeps the barrier low even though this audience can afford it. Docked on uniqueness because a brewery springfest is not a rare event, but the hyper-local location (East Cobb's own Round Trip) makes it more relevant than a generic Atlanta pick.

event_name: Kennesaw Concert Series: Boy Band Review
blurb: The Kennesaw Concert Series is back for its third year, and this one's bringing the Boy Band Review. Yes, THAT kind of boy band — the kind that defined middle school dances and road trip playlists for roughly a decade. It's nostalgia, it's outdoors, it's free, and it's close. If you grew up on NSYNC, BSB, or any variation of perfectly synchronized choreography, this is your night. Bring a chair, grab something from a food truck, and let the younger version of yourself have a moment.
scoring_notes: Median age of 42 means this audience was squarely in their prime during the boy band era of the late '90s and early 2000s — the nostalgia angle is a direct hit. Free, outdoor, community concert series fits the established homeowner profile perfectly. It's not a one-night-only headliner, but the specific theme elevates it above a generic 'free concert' pick. Strong demographic and audience match. Deducted slightly on uniqueness because the concert series itself is recurring.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Sundown Social Series – Sandy Springs**
📅 Returning this season | Sandy Springs
🎟️ Details at the link | [Learn More](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/24/the-future-of-sandy-springs)

The Sundown Social is back in Sandy Springs. It's an outdoor evening series — the kind of thing you show up to not quite knowing who you'll run into, and end up staying two hours longer than you planned.

For a neighborhood that's more young professionals and adults than it is school-night-bedtime households, this is exactly the right kind of event. Outdoor, social, low-commitment. You can walk in solo and leave with plans for the weekend.

Check Rough Draft Atlanta for the full schedule and dates so you don't miss the first one.
scoring_notes: The Sundown Social fits the adult-leaning, social Perimeter audience well. The series format is a slight knock on uniqueness — it's recurring — but the season return makes it timely for a newsletter publishing this week. Good match for young professionals and empty nesters who want something low-key and walkable. Lower uniqueness score reflects that it's a returning series rather than a standalone event, but the audience match is among the strongest of any candidate.

event_name: Mean Girls (The Musical) – City Springs Theatre Company
venue: Byers Theatre at City Springs, Sandy Springs
blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Mean Girls (The Musical) – City Springs Theatre Company**
📅 Opens Friday, May 1 | Byers Theatre at City Springs, Sandy Springs
🎟️ Tickets vary by seat | [Get Tickets](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/27/mean-girls-comes-to-sandy-springs-may-1/)

You already know the movie. You probably know the lines. But City Springs Theatre Company is bringing Mean Girls to the Byers stage as a full-blown musical — and it plays completely differently when it's live in front of you.

This is not a touring production passing through. This is your local theatre company putting it on right here in Sandy Springs. The writing is sharp, the songs are genuinely catchy, and it's the kind of show that works whether you're going with a girlfriend group, a date, or honestly just yourself on a weeknight.

It opens May 1, which means you can catch it before the whole neighborhood is talking about it. Grab your tickets at cityspringstheatre.com before the good seats go.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for an adult-skewing, educated, $105K median income audience in Sandy Springs. Mean Girls as a musical has broad appeal across the 30s–50s demographic — the movie is nostalgic for some and current-culture for others. It's a local production at a neighborhood venue, which adds a community angle. Not a one-night-only event, but it's a new production opening this week, which gives it timeliness. Family-skew is low (not really a kids show), which suits this more adult-leaning audience well.
